wrapperRole: Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper (natural or maduro) frames the ligero core with spice, earth, and oily depth without softening the strength.
binderRole: Dominican binder supports combustion and structure through a dense double-ligero bunch.
fillerRole: Dominican ligero filler drives exceptional nicotine, red pepper, leather, coffee, and experienced-only intensity.
flavorNotes: Red pepper, leather, charred oak, earth, coffee, dark spice, mineral tobacco.
bestFor: Experienced smoker, after dinner, rum pairing, user wants a Dominican power lesson—not the mild DR stereotype.
avoidIf: Beginner, nicotine-sensitive, empty stomach, morning coffee, user wants creamy Connecticut mildness.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user seeks maximum Dominican ligero intensity and understands this is among the strongest profiles in the book.

wrapperRole: Dominican sun-grown wrapper delivers OpusX signature spice, sweetness, and the prestige of an estate-grown puro cover leaf.
binderRole: Dominican binder integrates the puro structure and supports Fuente's precise, celebration-grade construction.
fillerRole: Dominican filler provides cedar, baking spice, dried fruit, and layered complexity without outside tobacco.
flavorNotes: Cedar, baking spice, leather, sweet tobacco, dried fruit, subtle cream, oak.
bestFor: Collector, Fuente/OpusX fan, experienced smoker, celebration, cognac pairing, Dominican puro prestige.
avoidIf: Beginner, nicotine-sensitive, budget-sensitive, user needs easy daily availability or a short smoke window.
whyRecommend: Recommend when prestige, rarity, and a refined full-bodied Dominican puro are the goal—not blunt ligero shock.

wrapperRole: Dominican sun-grown wrapper adds caramelized sweetness, graham, and restrained spice from Fuente estate leaf.
binderRole: Dominican binder keeps the all-Dominican blend coherent and elegantly constructed.
fillerRole: Dominican filler supplies cedar, molasses, cocoa, and medium-full body with signature Fuente polish.
flavorNotes: Graham cracker, cedar, cocoa, molasses, black pepper, toasted nuts, sweet spice.
bestFor: Celebration, port pairing, experienced smoker wanting luxury Dominican richness without OpusX collector pricing.
avoidIf: Beginner on empty stomach, user wants mild Connecticut cream only, or lowest nicotine.
whyRecommend: Recommend as a top-tier Dominican reserve—rich, smooth, and celebratory (often called the "velvet hammer").

wrapperRole: Cameroon wrapper contributes honeyed sweetness, baking spice, and aromatic texture unique to African leaf.
binderRole: Dominican binder supports figurado construction and even burn through shaped formats.
fillerRole: Dominican filler keeps the profile medium-bodied with cedar, earth, brown sugar, and approachable Fuente balance.
flavorNotes: Cedar, brown sugar, baking spice, toast, nuts, light earth, subtle cream.
bestFor: After dinner, rum pairing, craftsmanship/figurado lesson; Short Story for shorter window.
avoidIf: User dislikes figurados, wants full ligero power, or needs the lowest nicotine in the book.
whyRecommend: Recommend to teach Fuente figurado skill and Cameroon sweetness in a medium, approachable package.

wrapperRole: Connecticut Shade wrapper delivers cream, cedar, almond, and soft sweetness with premium Dominican polish.
binderRole: Dominican binder maintains smooth burn and Fuente-made consistency.
fillerRole: Dominican filler provides gentle body, nuts, light spice, and reliable mild-to-medium character.
flavorNotes: Cream, cedar, almonds, toast, light spice, faint sweetness.
bestFor: Beginner, morning coffee, mild luxury, user wants smooth Dominican Connecticut.
avoidIf: User wants pepper-forward power, dark maduro body, or collector scarcity story.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user needs a polished, beginner-safe Dominican Connecticut profile.

wrapperRole: Connecticut Shade wrapper gives grassy sweetness, cream, and classic Cuban-legacy mildness.
binderRole: Dominican binder supports approachable, consistent construction from Tabacalera de García.
fillerRole: Dominican filler reinforces cedar, nuts, light coffee, and easy-drinking mild body.
flavorNotes: Cream, cedar, nuts, grassy sweetness, light coffee, mild spice.
bestFor: Beginner, morning coffee, Cuban-heritage mild profile, first widely known premium classic.
avoidIf: User wants bold pepper, dark maduro, or boutique Nicaraguan intensity.
whyRecommend: Recommend as a widely available mild Dominican classic with broad beginner appeal.

wrapperRole: Ecuador Connecticut wrapper adds elegance, cream, and refined aromatic top notes.
binderRole: Dominican binder preserves Davidoff's clean construction and balanced mild character.
fillerRole: Dominican filler offers subtle nuts, cedar, coffee-with-cream, and low-intensity luxury complexity.
flavorNotes: Cedar, nuts, cream, coffee, soft spice, floral notes, light toast.
bestFor: Celebration, cognac pairing, luxury gift, user wants prestige without strength.
avoidIf: User equates luxury with full strength, wants pepper-heavy body, or is budget-sensitive.
whyRecommend: Recommend to show that prestige can be gentle, refined, and low-intensity.

wrapperRole: Cameroon wrapper adds wood, leather texture, and classic aromatic sweetness.
binderRole: Indonesian Jember binder adds supple body and savory depth under the Cameroon top leaf.
fillerRole: Dominican Piloto Cubano filler provides cedar, cream, black pepper, and medium Cuban-legacy character.
flavorNotes: Cedar, cream, black pepper, wood, leather, toast, subtle sweetness.
bestFor: After dinner, Scotch pairing, medium heritage prestige, recognizable brand without OpusX pricing.
avoidIf: User wants creamy mildness only, Cuban island product (clarify: Red Dot is DR-made), or lowest price point.
whyRecommend: Recommend for medium-bodied Dominican prestige with Cameroon texture—U.S.-legal alternative to Cuban Cohiba story.

wrapperRole: Nicaraguan Habano natural or maduro wrapper sets cocoa/coffee tone and teaches wrapper-driven sweetness vs depth.
binderRole: Nicaraguan binder reinforces puro identity and rustic, honest Padron construction.
fillerRole: Nicaraguan filler drives cocoa, earth, coffee, and dependable medium body—the classic daily Padron profile.
flavorNotes: Cocoa, coffee, earth, nuts, cedar, light pepper, sweet tobacco.
bestFor: Quick lunch break, coffee pairing, Nicaraguan baseline lesson, value-conscious premium smoker.
avoidIf: Beginner nicotine-sensitive, user wants Dominican cream or anniversary-line collector rarity.
whyRecommend: Recommend as the reference Nicaraguan puro for balanced body without 1964/1926 pricing.
notes: Padron portfolio — Entry tier (Thousands) — 2000 Robusto natural — aged 2.5–3 years — crisp wood, earth, cocoa, nuts. Natural wrapper sibling to CUR-43 maduro.

wrapperRole: Ecuadorian Habano Sun Grown wrapper adds oily sweetness, spice, and Jalapa/Estelí character on the nose.
binderRole: Nicaraguan binder supports full body and ligero-forward structure.
fillerRole: Nicaraguan ligero filler delivers espresso, pepper, dark cocoa, and long-finishing strength.
flavorNotes: Sweet spice, espresso, dark cocoa, cedar, leather, black pepper, earth.
bestFor: Experienced smoker, after dinner, bourbon pairing, user wants full Nicaraguan intensity with sweetness.
avoidIf: Beginner, empty stomach, short time window, user avoids high nicotine.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user asks for rich Nicaraguan power with sweetness and structure.

wrapperRole: Nicaraguan Habano Oscuro wrapper deepens cocoa, leather, and pepper with oily maduro texture.
binderRole: Nicaraguan binder (often double-binder bunch) amplifies Garcia-family structure and full-bodied burn.
fillerRole: Nicaraguan filler drives espresso, dark cocoa, earth, cherry-dark fruit, and experienced-only intensity.
flavorNotes: Dark cocoa, espresso, leather, black pepper, earth, dark fruit, charred wood.
bestFor: Experienced smoker, winter smoke, after-dinner Scotch, user wants dark Nicaraguan Garcia body.
avoidIf: Beginner, mild preference, morning smoke, user dislikes pepper and heavy body.
whyRecommend: Recommend for a serious, dark, full-bodied Nicaraguan profile with My Father blending identity.

wrapperRole: Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper brings fermented dark sweetness, cocoa, and dense oily texture.
binderRole: Mexican San Andrés binder adds earthy sweetness and savory depth under the broadleaf.
fillerRole: Nicaraguan and Honduran filler supply body, spice, espresso, and a broad dark flavor core.
flavorNotes: Dark cocoa, espresso, leather, earth, molasses, cedar, warming spice.
bestFor: Boutique fan, special occasion, bourbon pairing, user wants broadleaf richness with construction pedigree.
avoidIf: User wants mild cream, short smoke, low nicotine, or very simple Connecticut flavors.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user wants boutique broadleaf depth with strong identity and texture.

wrapperRole: San Andrés maduro wrapper contributes dark earth, cocoa, chicory, and savory sweetness.
binderRole: Ecuador Habano binder adds structure, spice lift, and cohesion for the complex filler stack.
fillerRole: Nicaraguan filler provides body, loam, espresso undertones, and evolving savory depth.
flavorNotes: Hot cocoa, leather, cream, earth, chicory, cedar, subtle sweetness, espresso.
bestFor: Boutique enthusiast, cognac pairing, experienced smoker wanting layered savory-maduro without blunt force.
avoidIf: Beginner, user wants mild Connecticut, or needs lowest price-point daily smoke.
whyRecommend: Recommend for no-compromise boutique balance—rich, savory, and nuanced rather than one-note strength.

wrapperRole: Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper delivers dark chocolate, espresso, and fermented sweetness with oily richness.
binderRole: Brazilian Mata Fina binder adds aromatic sweetness and savory depth distinctive to Liga blends.
fillerRole: Honduran and Nicaraguan filler provide earth, subtle barnyard, cocoa, and private-blend density.
flavorNotes: Dark chocolate, espresso, earth, leather, sweet tobacco, cedar, cocoa powder.
bestFor: Celebration, bourbon pairing, private-blend fan, experienced smoker, after-dinner richness.
avoidIf: Beginner, mild Connecticut preference, user dislikes earthy private-blend character.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user wants benchmark private-blend dark richness with Liga Privada pedigree.

avoidIf: User wants full body, maximum strength, bold espresso pairing, or a powerhouse smoke — Angel's Share is a softer, cream-forward OpusX sub-line, not standard OpusX strength.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user wants OpusX prestige with a sweeter, cream-forward profile — not maximum strength or bold bitter coffee.
bestFor: Celebration, cognac, experienced smoker wanting OpusX rarity without standard OpusX pepper bomb.
lineNote: Sub-line override: do not inherit Fuente OpusX Full-body rating — lower-priming wrapper, cream/baking-spice forward.
catalogLine: Arturo Fuente Fuente Fuente OpusX Angel's Share Reserva d'Chateau · 7 × 48 · Churchill · Medium · Dominican · $35 · Notes: cedar, baking spice, fruit, cream
displayName: Arturo Fuente Opus X Angel's Share

avoidIf: User wants full body, maximum strength, or Liga Privada No. 9–class broadleaf power — H99 is the Connecticut-hybrid sub-line, not the maduro flagship.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user wants Drew Estate complexity with Connecticut sweetness — not No. 9 strength.
bestFor: Bourbon or coffee pairing, experienced smoker wanting Liga complexity without broadleaf heaviness.
lineNote: Sub-line: Liga Privada H99 (CT Corojo hybrid) vs Liga Privada No. 9 (Broadleaf, Full).
catalogLine: Drew Estate Liga Privada H99 · 5 × 52 · Robusto · Medium · Nicaragua · $18 · Notes: earth, dark chocolate, espresso, apple pie, raisin

wrapperRole: Mexican San Andrés wrapper — dark, rugged leaf; cocoa, earth, and savory spice. Not Connecticut natural, not Nicaraguan puro.
binderRole: Nicaraguan binder supports combustion through the thick short-gordo format.
fillerRole: Dominican, Honduran, and Nicaraguan filler blend — not a puro. Blended by General Cigar Co., rolled at STG Estelí (Nicaragua).
flavorNotes: earth, savory spice, cedar, cocoa, dark sweetness
bestFor: After dinner, bourbon pairing, experienced smoker who wants a Best Buy with San Andrés weight — not a morning coffee cigar.
avoidIf: Morning coffee or espresso (60-ring gordo outlasts the cup; heavy wrapper coats the palate). Beginner, nicotine-sensitive, user wants mild Connecticut or a true puro story.
whyRecommend: Recommend for value seekers citing CA 2024 Best Buy — Lunar New Year Dragon Fire short gordo at ~$7, General Cigar / STG Estelí production.
memberBlurb: Punch Dragon Fire — General Cigar blend, STG Estelí. Mexican San Andrés over Nicaraguan binder and a Dominican/Honduran/Nicaraguan filler stack. ~$7 short gordo (5 × 60), flat head. After-dinner strength, not a morning smoke.
catalogLine: Punch Dragon Fire · 5 × 60 · Short Gordo · Medium-Full · Nicaragua · $7 · Notes: earth, savory spice, cedar, cocoa
lineNote: Lunar New Year limited Dragon Fire — single vitola 5 × 60 short gordo, flat head, MSRP ~$6.99. General Cigar Co. blend, STG Estelí factory. NOT Plasencia; NOT Nicaraguan puro; NOT natural Connecticut wrapper; NOT offered as Robusto.

notes: Padron portfolio — Premium tier (1964) — Exclusivo maduro — 4-year box-pressed. Intense cocoa, molasses, dark coffee. Natural sibling: CUR-44-PAD-1964-EX-NAT.

lineNote: Sub-line override: only Padron with Connecticut Shade — never inherit 1926/1964 Full-body defaults.
avoidIf: User wants full Nicaraguan puro power, 1926 pepper bomb, or maduro sweetness only.
whyRecommend: Recommend when user wants Padron prestige with Connecticut mildness — creamy wildcard in Padron-only flights.
